summary: Re-linked the player module by changing `melee/pl/player.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`, with small source/header cleanups that preserved matching behavior while reducing previous MUST_MATCH and clang warning scaffolding.
what_changed: `configure.py` now marks `Object(Matching, "melee/pl/player.c")` instead of `Object(NonMatching, "melee/pl/player.c")` under the `pl (Player)` library.
`Player_SetFlagsAEBit1` was made conditionally `void` under `BUGFIX` and `u8` otherwise, matching the header declaration.

`Player_SetFlagsAEBit1` was rewritten to declare a `StaticPlayer* player`, assign it after `Player_CheckSlot(slot)`, and write `player->flagsAE.b1 = bit1` instead of writing directly through `player_slots[slot]`.
Removed a prior `MUST_MATCH`-guarded unused assignment block and clang `-Wreturn-type` diagnostic suppression around `Player_SetFlagsAEBit1`.
In `Player_80036F34` and `Player_80037054`, the dummy `u8 _;` local is now unconditional rather than wrapped in `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`.
`player.h` replaced the comment saying the function output is actually void but needs `u8` to match with a `BUGFIX`-guarded prototype split.
smart_moves: Used a `BUGFIX` conditional to express the likely-correct `void` signature while preserving the non-bugfix `u8` ABI/source shape needed for matching.

Removed clang diagnostic pragmas by arranging the non-bugfix implementation to omit an explicit return from a non-void function, preserving the original behavior without local warning-control clutter.
Turned match-only dummy locals in `Player_80036F34` and `Player_80037054` into regular declarations, making the source closer to the matched form and reducing `MUST_MATCH` noise.
Kept the actual matching-status flip limited to `player.c` in `configure.py`, indicating the cleanup was enough to re-link this translation unit without broad system changes.
decomp_lessons: When a function appears to have a HAL typo or ABI mismatch, prefer documenting it structurally with `#ifdef BUGFIX` prototypes and definitions rather than relying only on comments.
A non-void function with no return may be intentional for matching; if so, isolate the corrected signature under `BUGFIX` and preserve the matching signature in the default build.

Unused locals or temporary pointer variables can be required for register allocation or stack/codegen shape; once confirmed, keeping them unconditionally may be cleaner than hiding them behind `MUST_MATCH`.

Relinking a module can require small source-shape cleanups even when behavior is unchanged; the important project-level step is updating `configure.py` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` only after the object is verified.
naming_conventions: Player API functions retain the `Player_` prefix and descriptive suffixes such as `GetFlagsAEBit1`, `SetFlagsAEBit1`, `GetUnk4C`, and `SetUnk4C`.
Unknown fields remain named with `Unk` plus offset-style suffixes, e.g. `Unk4C` and `Unk4D`.
Static player storage uses `StaticPlayer* player` and `player_slots[slot]` naming.
Bug-corrected variants are guarded with the project-wide `BUGFIX` macro rather than renaming the function.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed direct indexed field assignment to a local `StaticPlayer* player` followed by `player->flagsAE.b1 = bit1`, likely to match compiler codegen/register usage.
Preserved a non-returning `u8` function signature in the default build for matching, while allowing a corrected `void` signature under `BUGFIX`.
Kept dummy `u8 _;` locals in `Player_80036F34` and `Player_80037054`, suggesting stack layout or codegen sensitivity.
Removed `MUST_MATCH` guards after the match-specific constructs became accepted as part of the canonical source for the matching build.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that remove or revise the `BUGFIX` conditional around `Player_SetFlagsAEBit1`.
Search for other relink PRs that convert `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` after removing `MUST_MATCH` scaffolding.
Check whether `Player_80036F34` and `Player_80037054` dummy locals were later named or eliminated after more type/struct recovery.
